qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated remote code execution via auto-login endpoint and unsafe code validation exec sink
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes unauthenticated RCE via AUTO_LOGIN + exec() in /validate/code. The advisory describes IDOR in delete_api_key_route() due to missing ownership verification. The vulnerable code locations, root cause, and vulnerability class are entirely different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes an IDOR vulnerability in delete_api_key_route() (api_key.py:44-53) where the delete_api_key() CRUD function does not verify API key ownership before deletion. The sole qualifying finding describes unauthenticated RCE via AUTO_LOGIN + exec() in /validate/code, which is a completely different endpoint, root cause, and vulnerability class.
evidence: Advisory root cause: delete_api_key_route() in api_key.py:44-53 accepts api_key_id and calls delete_api_key(db, api_key_id) without verifying api_key.user_id == current_user.id.
unauth_rce_via_auto_login targets login.py:87-124 and validate.py:13-16 — completely different endpoint and root cause.
The qualifying finding does not mention api_key.py, delete_api_key_route, delete_api_key, or any ownership verification gap.
